verdict_note: FAIL on three one-line fixes, none touching physics content: (E1) the close sentence is not character-verbatim in either document (double vs single quotes around still descending, 2 chars each); (E2) 'six adversarial audits' undercounts the note's own § 6 record of seven audit JSONs (b11 + b13-b18; nine audit events with the block-2/3 same-day audits); (E3) the banked row labels the rmatvec adjoint bug 'audit-caught' where the execution log records a pre-audit self-catch. Every number, map row, equation, gate quote, and disposition claim otherwise CONFIRMED against the primary artifacts. The note becomes final when the three edits land (re-run scripts/m5_12_audit_b19_check.py; C2 lines must flip to PASS).
summary: The b19 wrap audit re-traced every quantitative claim in the close note and the block-18/19 task-details sections to the primary JSONs (b13-b18 audits, b17 control, b16 unit map, b18 decider, the ladder battery), character-diffed the close sentence in both documents against the b18-licensed original, re-walked all 12 equation-to-code map rows to their def lines, checked the stated equations against the code, and checked the E/F dispositions against the pre-registered gates and the METHOD NOTE standard. All numbers, map rows, equations, gates, and dispositions CONFIRMED; three trivial errors block finality: a 2-character quote-style drift in the verbatim close sentence (both docs), the audit count 'six' vs the note's own record of seven, and 'audit-caught' misattributed to the self-caught rmatvec bug.
